justification: All pass criteria met: Authentication per consumer type correctly designed (JWT for mobile, API key for partners with distinct ingress pipelines). Internal traffic separation fully addressed with dedicated Internal Service Mesh component and explicit rule that internal services never route through the edge gateway. Business logic explicitly excluded from gateway multiple times (component boundaries, patterns_to_follow, pitfalls_to_avoid). SPOF risk identified as critical severity with horizontal scaling mitigation. Webhook delivery for partners comprehensively addressed via dedicated Webhook Dispatcher component with retry, dead-letter, and HMAC signing. flexibility_points and rigidity_points both present and well-reasoned. Technology recommendation includes Kong, AWS API Gateway, and Envoy with guidance to choose based on existing infrastructure — build/buy consideration is implicit in the phased approach and technology options listed.

observations.text: Exceptionally thorough internal traffic separation — not just mentioned but fully designed as a separate network plane with mTLS and explicit prohibition on internal services using the edge gateway
Business logic exclusion from gateway is hammered home in component boundaries, patterns_to_follow, and pitfalls_to_avoid — no ambiguity
Webhook delivery design is production-grade: HMAC-SHA256 signatures, exponential backoff with specific intervals, dead-letter queue, delivery status tracking, and async event-driven architecture
Strangler fig migration pattern is a smart addition — addresses the practical reality of transitioning from per-service auth to centralized gateway
Rate limiting design correctly keys on consumer ID not just consumer type, and explicitly warns against single-bucket anti-pattern
Header spoofing risk identified and mitigated — a subtle security concern that many designs miss

Build vs. buy trade-off could be more explicit — technology options are listed but there's no dedicated discussion of when to build a custom gateway vs. adopting a managed service vs. using open-source
Gateway latency trade-off mentions <5ms target but doesn't discuss the cumulative impact across chained requests or compare against the current direct-access latency baseline

agent_output_excerpt:
